data.epi_content:
I turned the doorknob repeatedly, but it wouldn't open.

Magicians create their own spells to use as keys to open and close doors, don't they?

I guess Vivian does that too. Or maybe she just locked it with a regular key.

"Um, were you actually trying to escape by just running out of here?"

Evan speaks as if he doesn't know whether to laugh or cry.

Even I think that if someone flipped a table right in front of me, I'd be angry too, but it would be kind of funny if they stupidly couldn't open the door to get out.

As if someone like me could escape just by flipping a table and running to the entrance.

It's common sense that when a door is locked, turning the doorknob won't open it.

I'd forgotten that since I haven't been living with closed doors lately.

"Um, well... what should I say."

The two of them stared at me intently.

How uncomfortable.

"...Yeah."

I answer in a small voice.

But how do I deal with emotions that override rational thoughts in the moment?

It feels like a kind of instinct.

Glancing behind me, I saw Vivian setting the table I had flipped back up and cleaning the room with a spell.

"It's not even your room, it's someone else's.

I didn't think you'd ever do something like this."

Evan says while scratching his chin slightly.

"I wouldn't have. But what does that matter now?

The place that shaped those behaviors has completely collapsed, leaving nothing behind."

"When you do something wrong, shouldn't you apologize rather than taking this attitude? To Vivian, of course."

Hearing this, Vivian frantically tries to stop Evan.

As if to say I would never apologize in the first place.

But I already got down on my knees and begged, saying I was wrong.

Or was I just asking to be let go?

Anyway, my pride has been completely crushed beyond recognition, so there's no need for me to explain it all.

"Evan, it's okay! Actually..."

I cut off whatever Vivian was about to say and continue.

"Actually, it's your fault for forcibly bringing me here, tying me up, and not letting me go. Kidnapping and imprisonment."

Hearing this, Vivian slightly turns her eyes away, avoiding my gaze.

I just sit down on the marble floor where shoes are put on at the entrance.

My bottom felt cold.

"Well, it's not unreasonable for someone's attitude to change overnight like this.

I'm fine. So just leave me alone."

"That's what everyone in a mental hospital says, except the doctors."

It's called a mental hospital, but it's really more like a detention center where they might not even feed you properly.

A place where they'll lock people up within legal limits if you pay them enough.

"What, are you going to lock me up?"

"That's not what I meant."

"So what. Can you let me out of here?

It's suffocating. Look! I'm perfectly fine now.

I'm not in pain, and I'm not sad either."

Vivian still has the gun, but dying isn't limited to blowing your head off.

"No. It's not appropriate for you to stay in the same room with a man, so stay with Vivian."

Saying this, Evan lifts me with telekinesis and places me on the bed.

The experience of floating in the air, flailing my arms and legs without being able to move, was quite unpleasant yet novel.

"Vivian, you can fit another bed in here, right?"

"Ah, yes! But I don't have money, could you lend me some?"
data.epi_content2:
Why are you two deciding everything on your own?

"Well, I'll buy one for you, so don't worry.

Please take care of Erica. You must be tired too, but Erica must be going through a lot.

Oh, and if anything happens, call me."

Saying this, Evan levitates two gems from the ring he had given me before and begins to chant a spell.

Then he gives one to Vivian and the other to me.

"If you break it, I'll come right away."

Evan says this and leaves the room.

Looking at Vivian's face, she seemed slightly smitten.

"This is a disaster."

"...Erica."

"What, did you find a pet that will wag its tail and act cute if you lock it up, feed it, and show it affection?"

"No matter what you say to me, you're staying here.

At least there are no blades here, and no guns loaded with bullets."

Hearing that, I pulled my legs together on the bed and hugged my knees.

I buried my face and looked down at the darkness rather than ahead.

"This is utterly unpleasant."

"......"

They didn't give me any attention and left me to wither away.

Now that they see me dying before their eyes, they finally act like this?

I suppose there's no way to know when a person's heart is dying.

Still, they could have shown a little concern.

Then my mind wouldn't have gotten mixed up with this pitiful human...

But I guess there's no point in saying anything now.

There's no meaning in repeating what's already been said.

"Vivian, how are you going to handle my classes?"

"Either I or Evan will escort you to the classroom."

"And when class ends?"

"Similarly, the two of us will come find you.

And maybe if you wander around the school like before, things might get a little better."

"Me? Or our relationship?"

"Both."

"I don't know."

"Anyway, rest for today.

Missing a few days won't cause any major problems for you."

"What about you?"

"Evan helped me."

"Well, that's..."

I almost said I was envious.

"It's nothing."

..............................

I began a strange cohabitation with Vivian.

It feels like I've become a pet being raised.

Since being confined here, I've just sat on the bed doing nothing, spacing out.

Occasionally Vivian would bring tea and cookies which I enjoyed, but that was it.

My daily routine consisted of sitting blankly, almost as if I wasn't human.
data.epi_content3:
Looking out the window, my gaze was directed outside, but my mind was elsewhere.

Vivian must have noticed this and asked me a question.

"Erica, what are you thinking about?"

"Just, old memories."

After my early elementary school years, even when I was just starting to grow up, my mother always stuck by my side and took me to school.

The route was safe, and all the children walked together, so I would speak to my mom in a sulky tone when she accompanied me.

I'd ask why she was following me when I was old enough.

She said she just wanted to be with me, and while it was too close to drive and there were too many people around, she just wanted to walk beside me.

I remember nodding and saying, "Is that so?"

After entering middle school, she did drive me, but I remember more clearly walking to school with her right beside me.

And it remains a rather good memory.

After all, it's a memory of being with family I got along with, not someone I disliked.

Sometimes I miss it.

If you ask why I'm bringing this up, it's because I'm in a similar but completely different situation. It just came to mind.

"Let's go now."

But now it's truly dreadful.

Someone who isn't family, isn't close to me, and who forcibly brought me here and confined me despite my resistance, sticks by my side, takes me to the classroom, and then picks me up again.

"So, you're taking me again?"

Vivian nodded, seeming apologetic herself.

Even if I tried to ask someone for help, no one comes to mind.

The teachers have probably already been silenced by Evan, and the higher-ranking individuals are likely holed up in their labs doing experiments all day rather than coming here to teach, so I won't run into them.

Thanks to this, I'm now being ridiculed in the classroom.

"Lady Erica, did you replace your mother with a commoner magician because you couldn't accept that vulgar and lustful woman as your mother?

Well, even though the rumors are similar, she's much more capable, so it seems like a good method!"

"Indeed."

"Right? Honestly, aren't you a bit ashamed?

With your family completely ruined, you're parasitically clinging to a commoner.

I couldn't imagine living such a life."

When I didn't respond, Lydia stared at me intensely for quite some time, pressuring me.

After staring at me for nearly two minutes, did she get bored?

She smiled once and then slapped my cheek.

My head turned.

There's probably a handprint on my face right now.

But judging by the slight stinging sensation, it won't leave a mark.

Just a slight redness.

"React a little, will you? It's boring when I'm talking all by myself."

Right now, I wanted to pull out a gun and blow someone's head off.

Either mine or Lydia's, one of the two.

"How long are you going to keep this up?"

"At least as long as we're attending the Academy together, I should have fun with you.

We may not have the will or ability to learn, but thanks to not having a stick between our legs, we're stuck here.

To meet proper men."

Lydia says this, takes a breath, and then gives a mischievous smile.

"Ah, that commoner already took you, didn't he? How pitiful.

Well, I'm being careful not to hit anywhere visible to avoid trouble."

When I glared at her, imagining blowing her head off, she lightly tapped my head with her hand.

"Let's end it here for today!"

Lydia left the classroom with the other students, smiling.

After some time, I also left the classroom.

Vivian was waiting in front.

As if it were the most natural thing.
